name: Contecnica: Landing platform
description: Production monorepo with FastAPI, PostgreSQL, Redis, and Next.js App Router; admin edits publish to the DB and invalidate the public cache over WebSocket.
longDescription: Contecnica’s marketing stack is built as a production-oriented monorepo: an async FastAPI backend (SQLAlchemy 2, PostgreSQL, Redis, Alembic) and a Next.js App Router frontend (TypeScript, shadcn/ui, Motion). Content changes are written through the API first; the system broadcasts typed WebSocket invalidation so the public app re-fetches authoritative JSON. Docker/Podman workflows, env documentation, and release bundles (including GHCR-oriented packaging) support repeatable deploys.
features: REST API with async SQLAlchemy and migrations via Alembic
Next.js public site with server/client boundaries and typed content refetch
Redis-backed notification path for content-change fan-out
WebSocket channel with origin checks and production TLS guidance
Docker Compose for local and production-shaped stacks
Release packaging scripts for on-server build and GHCR image pulls
Windows manage.ps1 / Node TUI for common operations
highlights: Single source of truth in the database with explicit cache invalidation
Clear split between public Next.js and FastAPI documentation surface
Operational scripts aligned with container-first hosting
challenges: Marketing sites that mix CMS-like workflows with static performance need consistent cache semantics and safe realtime updates without stale public JSON.
solutions: Use DB-first writes, Redis for fan-out, and a client that refetches on invalidation while keeping production WebSocket and origin configuration documented next to compose files.
security: Origin-scoped WebSocket usage documented for dev vs production
Separate public vs admin concerns in API design
Secrets live only on deployment targets; source control carries env templates without live values
